question: Which part of the {{target}} should we trim to reduce token cost?
plain_language_preface: We're about to trim {{target}} to cut down size and cost. Choosing the right cut point avoids breaking callers while hitting the token budget.

options.label: Drop oldest entries first (time-ordered trim)
options.tradeoff: Preserves recent context; loses historical audit trail.

options.label: Drop lowest-weight entries (score-ordered trim)
options.tradeoff: Preserves high-signal entries; scoring may be imprecise.

options.label: Drop duplicate / near-duplicate entries (dedup trim)
options.tradeoff: Safe — zero loss of unique signal; may under-trim.

options.label: Truncate tail to hard byte cap
options.tradeoff: Deterministic; may cut a sentence mid-token.

recommended_rationale: Dedup trim has zero information loss risk and is reversible; choose score-ordered (B) only when a reliable weight signal exists.
